ile removes the duplicate so there's only one source of hook truth.

Safer alternative: instead of deleting, empty out its hooks object so the file structure remains as a placeholder for any future di-specific settings:

```json
{ "hooks": {} }
```

I AM GUESSING you'll prefer outright deletion since cleanup implies "remove the dead weight". Confirm with "go" (or pick the alternative and say so).

tates across the three inputs.

**Question.** Do you want me to (a) leave face-label as-is since the visible reactivity already works, or (b) change face-label to use the same in-flight pattern (only write to the saved name on commit, like the other two)? Option b is more invasive — it would change undo behavior and the live-update of any other thing that reads so.name on every keystroke today.

ing b):
- The parts list table will no longer follow face-label typing live; it will refresh on commit.
- The drawn face name on the cube face won't follow live either; it refreshes on commit.
- Undo history will record one snapshot per commit (consistent with the other two inputs) instead of one per keystroke.

**Scope.** One file: Face_Label.ts. No template changes.

Confirm with "go" to apply.

caught at end of turn — Claude doesn't see them until next turn. For long turns that introduce errors early and don't notice, that's a delay in catching them. I AM GUESSING the latency win outweighs this since Claude tends to run svelte-check anyway before claiming work done.

Order I'd apply: (a) first as a safety net, then (b) for the cascade fix, then (c) if you want maximum responsiveness.

Order I'd apply: (a) first as a safety net, then (b) for the cascade fix, then (c) if you want maximum responsiveness.

settings.local.json: replace check-ts.sh in PostToolUse with mark-ts-check-pending.sh; add check-ts.sh to Stop alongside banned-words-check and phrase-check.

Net effect: per-edit latency drops to a single `touch` call (~10ms). svelte-check runs at most once per turn, only when .ts/.svelte was edited, and never blocks even on errors. Errors surface as context on the next turn.

Say "go" to apply.

settings.local.json: replace check-ts.sh in PostToolUse with mark-ts-check-pending.sh; add check-ts.sh to Stop alongside banned-words-check and phrase-check.

Net effect: per-edit latency drops to a single `touch` call (~10ms). svelte-check runs at most once per turn, only when .ts/.svelte was edited, and never blocks even on errors. Errors surface as context on the next turn.

Say "go" to apply.

pick up the new settings.local.json wiring.

After the reload:
- Edit/Write turns should feel snappy again. Per-edit overhead is now a touch call.
- svelte-check runs once at end of turn for turns that touched .ts/.svelte files, capped at 30 seconds, never blocks the response.
- If errors are present, they arrive as context on your next turn (the same way the "PLAIN ENGLISH RULE" reminder does).

lush, the canvas's top corners are hidden by the top band and the bottom corners are hidden by the bottom band — so the visual rounding only shows on the four corners IF there's a gap or if the bands are narrower than the canvas. Worth a quick decision before I edit. Default I'd pick: small gap (matching the gap value already used elsewhere in the layout) so all four canvas corners are visible.

ph.svelte](di/src/lib/svelte/main/Graph.svelte):

1. Drop `!show_names &&` from the popup's render condition.
2. Drop the now-unused `show_names` derived.
3. Drop `w_decorations` from the stores destructure.
4. Drop `T_Decorations` from the imports (no longer referenced).

The popup will then show whenever there's a hover-result, regardless of whether names are drawn on faces.

Say "go" to apply.
